tldr: This is not an engine swap — it's adopting FMOD as the audio middleware inside Unreal, replacing Unreal's built-in audio system with FMOD's event-driven workflow.
why_switch: FMOD's event-driven authoring is friendlier to dedicated audio designers and composers
Adaptive music and parameterised mixing are first-class in FMOD vs hand-rolling in Unreal audio
Cross-platform parity for audio behavior across Switch, mobile, console, and PC
Free for indie revenue under FMOD's published indie threshold — verify current limit in their docs
Live update during playtests speeds iteration
what_to_export: Master list of every sound, music cue, and ambience used in the project
Source WAV / lossless audio assets (not the cooked Unreal versions)
Mix snapshots and bus / submix structure from Unreal's audio mixer
Cue triggers — which Blueprints / C++ classes call which sounds
export_method: Gather original source audio from your DAW / asset library — not the Unreal-imported assets. Document every Blueprint/C++ call site that triggers a sound (search for PlaySound, PlaySoundAtLocation, AudioComponent, etc. in the Unreal project). Export mix bus structure and attenuation settings as notes; there is no automated cross-tool migration. Consult the official Unreal Engine and FMOD documentation for current integration plugin versions.
import_method: Install the FMOD Studio integration plugin for Unreal, build your event hierarchy in FMOD Studio, replace Unreal's audio cue calls with FMOD event posts in Blueprints/C++, and reroute spatialisation through FMOD's listener and emitter components. Keep Unreal's built-in audio for UI sounds initially if useful, then migrate fully once the FMOD pipeline is proven. Verify the integration plugin version matches your Unreal Engine version.

concept_mapping.note: FMOD events wrap multiple sources, parameters, and logic per cue.
Recreate hierarchy in FMOD Studio mixer.
Replace call sites; FMOD plugin exposes Blueprint nodes.
Define per event in FMOD Studio.
Drive via parameter from gameplay state.

gotchas: Confirm the FMOD Studio Unreal integration version supports your engine version before committing
Audio designers need FMOD Studio installed and trained — schedule ramp-up time
Indie pricing thresholds change — verify current limits in FMOD's official pricing page
Live update / network profiling requires consistent build setup across team machines
Plan a parallel period where built-in audio still works for UI while events migrate
Final mix needs full re-validation; QA passes are non-trivial

email.subject: Adopting FMOD for audio in our Unreal project
email.body:
Hi team,

Proposal: replace Unreal's built-in audio with FMOD as our audio middleware. To be clear, this is not an engine change — Unreal stays — we're swapping the audio layer.

Why: FMOD gives our composers/sound designers an event-driven authoring tool, faster iteration via live update, and consistent cross-platform behavior. Pricing remains friendly at our revenue scale (we'll re-confirm against FMOD's current published terms).

Plan:
1) Install the FMOD Studio integration plugin matching our Unreal version.
2) Inventory every sound, mix bus, attenuation, and call site in the project.
3) Build the event hierarchy in FMOD Studio.
4) Replace audio calls in Blueprints/C++ in vertical slices, validating each.
5) Migrate the final mix and decommission built-in audio paths.

Estimated effort: 2-8 weeks depending on cue count. I'll send a more detailed runbook before we start.

Thanks,
